module_map.responsibility: Entry point; CLI parsing via clap and config file loading; orchestrates initialization of Core.
Main orchestrator; manages file fetching, sorting, and display rendering. Coordinates between flags, meta collection, and display modules.
Converts Meta objects to visual output; handles grid and tree layouts; renders blocks with colors and icons.
File metadata extraction and caching; root module that aggregates all file system properties.
CLI flags and configuration; defines all display and behavior options.
Color theme management and element-to-color mapping; provides colorization utilities.
Icon resolution for file types; maps file characteristics to icon glyphs.
Sorting logic; assembles sorter functions and applies comparisons.
Git status caching and retrieval for files.
Color and icon theme definitions; loads and manages theme configurations.

data_flow.description: Parse CLI args with clap::Parser, load config file if specified, merge into Flags.
Initialize Colors, Icons, GitTheme, and sorters based on flags. Store in Core struct.

Collect file metadata via fs::symlink_metadata() and fs::metadata(); construct sub-objects for each property (Owner, Permissions, Size, Date, INode, Links, Name, FileType, SymLink, Indicator, AccessControl).
If directory and recursion enabled, walk directory tree and build nested Meta.content; query GitCache for git status on each file.
Apply configured sorters (sort_order) to Meta list using assembled SortFn callbacks.

For each Meta, iterate through flags.blocks and call get_output() which routes to render methods (inode.render(), size.render(), date.render(), name.render(), etc.). Each render method uses Colors for colorization and Icons for glyph resolution. Build grid cells and format as output.
Write formatted output string to stdout via macro that handles pipe failures.

cross_module_hubs.reason: Orchestrates rendering of all data: imports from core (Flags, Colors, Icons, GitTheme), meta (Meta, FileType, OwnerCache), flags (Block, Display, Layout, HyperlinkOption), color (Elem, Colors), icon (Icons). Routes each Meta/Block combination to specific render methods. Central nexus between data representation and visual output.

Aggregates all file properties via submodules (Name, Size, Date, Owner, Permissions, FileType, SymLink, INode, Links, Indicator, AccessControl, git_file_status). Core.rs calls Meta::from_path(); display.rs reads from Meta fields. Imports Flags for permission handling, GitCache for git status. Hub for filesystem state.

Central configuration hub: Core.rs instantiates Flags and passes to display.rs, sort.rs, meta recursion, color/icon initialization. Block enum drives the display.rs rendering loop. TryFrom<&str> parser integrates CLI args and config file into Flags. Flags affects almost every downstream decision.

change_proposal.objective: Add a SHA-256 file checksum display column
files_to_modify: src/flags/blocks.rs - Add Block::Checksum enum variant and TryFrom parsing
src/flags.rs - Add Checksum flag variant if not already handled by blocks
src/meta/mod.rs - Add checksum: Option<Checksum> field to Meta struct
src/meta/checksum.rs (new) - New module to compute and cache SHA-256 hashes
src/display.rs - Add Block::Checksum match arm in get_output() function
src/app.rs - Add --checksum CLI flag if needed (or use --blocks checksum)
new_types: pub struct Checksum { hash: String } - Wraps SHA-256 hash string with render() method
pub enum ChecksumAlgo { SHA256 } - Extensible for future hash algorithms
ChecksumCache - Optional cache to avoid re-hashing same files across runs (future optimization)
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Follow Size and Date modules: implement From<&std::fs::Metadata> + render(colors: &Colors) -> String. Store in Meta.checksum as Option<Checksum>. In display.rs get_output(), match Block::Checksum and call checksum.render(colors). Use Block layout pattern (header, padding rules, color element mapping).
change_proposal.integration_point: 1. Meta::from_path() calls Checksum::from(&metadata) to compute hash during file collection (can be lazy-computed only if Block::Checksum is in flags.blocks.0 to avoid overhead). 2. display.rs get_output() adds Block::Checksum arm alongside Block::Size. 3. flags/blocks.rs Block enum + TryFrom<&str> enables --blocks checksum CLI option. 4. Theme color mapping: define new Elem::Checksum in color.rs for hash string coloring.
risks: Performance: SHA-256 computation on every file is expensive. Mitigation: (1) Only compute if Block::Checksum in flags; (2) Consider optional lazy evaluation or caching; (3) Add --checksum-cache flag to cache hashes per run.
Large files: Reading entire file into memory for hashing can OOM on multi-GB files. Mitigation: Stream hash computation (sha2 crate supports incremental updates via Digest trait).
Terminal width: Long hash string (64 hex chars) competes with limited terminal width. Mitigation: Allow --checksum-truncate or column width configuration similar to Size block.
Cross-platform: File hashing may differ on Windows if line endings are normalized. Ensure binary-mode reading to avoid this.
Breaking change: Adding Block::Checksum expands the Block enum. Existing configs with --blocks won't break, but binary size increases slightly.
change_proposal.example_usage: lsd --long --blocks permission,user,group,size,checksum,date,name would display checksum column between size and date. Short hashes could use truncate-owner pattern for display control.

tool_usage.params: 1. max_depth=2 on /tmp/lsd-benchmark-target (overview); 2. max_depth=3 on src/ (file tree); 3. focus=from_path on meta/mod.rs (call chains); 4. Examined display.rs, core.rs, flags/blocks.rs manually to trace render pipeline.
view commands to read source files and understand data flow (main.rs, core.rs, display.rs, meta/mod.rs, flags/blocks.rs)

notes: lsd is a columnar file lister with pluggable blocks (permission, user, group, size, date, name, inode, links, git, context). Architecture follows Unix philosophy: metadata collection (meta/) → sorting (sort.rs) → rendering (display.rs) → output. Checksum would fit naturally as a new Block type, following existing Size/Date/INode patterns. Main risk is I/O performance; streaming hash computation with early-exit on Checksum-not-enabled is critical. No shared mutable state concerns for checksums (read-only file access, no cache write conflicts in single-threaded main flow).
